market_context: US stock futures are pointing to a lower open on March 20, with March E-mini S&P futures (ESH26) at 6,582.00 (-0.41%) and Nasdaq futures (NQH26) down -0.27% pre-market amid rising oil prices from Middle East tensions and inflation fears pushing bond yields higher. Major indices closed lower on March 19: S&P 500 -0.43%, Dow -0.59%, Nasdaq 100 -0.44%, with notable pre-market declines in META (-1.46%), MU (-3.78%), and MOS (-5.69%). VIX futures show elevated volatility, up 15.39% over the past month.

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 closed down -0.43% on March 19 amid inflation fears and higher bond yields (ZNM26 -0.20%), with E-mini S&P futures (ESH26) at 6,582.00 (-0.41%) signaling continued weakness into Friday's open. Pre-market shows broad declines including META (-1.46%) and MU (-3.78%), supporting further downside for SPY from recent levels.

Nasdaq 100 down -0.44% on March 19, with futures (NQH26) -0.27% pre-market amid oil price advances and Middle East escalation. Tech names like MU (-3.78%), NVDA (-1.02%), and MSFT (-0.71%) are dragging, likely pressuring QQQ lower by end of day.

Dow closed -0.59% on March 19 with futures (YRH26) showing monthly -7.04% decline from peaks, amid retreating stocks and rising yields. Pre-market weakness in industrials and broad market supports modest further drop.

MU at 444.27 (-3.78%) in pre-market, part of Nasdaq weakness with futures down -0.27% and elevated VIX futures (+15.39% monthly). Semiconductor sector pressure from broader tech retreat likely extends losses.
